summary: A high‑energy 1984 Berkeley show captured in a solid matrix mix, spotlighting the crushing Terrapin > Playing in the Band > China Doll sequence.
review: Fans rave about the dark, heavy second‑set jam—Terrapin into Playing in the Band and China Doll—plus tight Playing In The Band, Space and standout drums. The recording blends a crisp soundboard feed with occasional audience patches, resulting in an overall clear and enjoyable listening experience despite minor imperfections.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently enthusiastic reviews (mostly 4‑5 stars) praise both the musical performance and the recording quality; minor audience patches keep the technical rating just below “excellent.”

recording_quality.technical_notes: Soundboard is praised as crisp and well‑balanced, with a few audience patches noted but no jarring cuts; overall listenable and clear.

standout_songs: Terrapin > Playing in the Band > China Doll
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is described as tight and flowing, with an especially powerful second‑set jam that builds into a dark, heavy climax; the intimate venue adds to the vibe.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Riffing on J.S. Bach during the space and wailing on every song; vocals noted as past their prime but still solid.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass playing described as 'on' and a key part of the overall wailing sound.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drumming highlighted as top‑notch, especially in the second‑set jam and space.
